nodes.description: HIV infects and destroys CD4 T-cells, critical for immune system function.

evidence.reference_title: CD4(+) T-cell depletion in HIV infection: mechanisms of immunological failure.
evidence.snippet: The hallmark of acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS) pathogenesis is a progressive depletion of CD4(+) T-cell populations in close association with progressive impairment of cellular immunity and increasing susceptibility to opportunistic infections (OI).

evidence.reference_title: Overview of HIV and AIDS: biology and epidemiology of the virus.
evidence.snippet: HIV-1 infects mononuclear cells using the CD4+ molecule... ongoing cellular destruction, leading to the characteristic immunodeficiency of AIDS and its opportunistic infections and neoplasms.
The entry of one HIV virion into a human being has the potential to cause death by the inexorable replication of the virus within the principal T lymphocyte, the CD4+ T cell.

evidence.reference_title: New insights on the perturbations of T cell cycle during HIV infection.
evidence.snippet: The role of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) in the pathogenesis of the Acquired Immune-Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS) is changed. Direct HIV-mediated killing of CD4(+) T cells is not the only mechanism leading to lymphocyte depletion.

evidence.reference_title: The Role of Glutathione in the Management of Cell-Mediated Immune Responses in Individuals with HIV.
evidence.snippet: HIV is a major cause of death worldwide... HIV-positive individuals also demonstrate diminished glutathione (GSH) levels which allows for increased viral replication and...slowed the decline of CD4+ T cell counts in HIV-positive individuals.

nodes.description: HIV invades the CNS early, establishing persistent reservoirs in microglia and macrophages that contribute to neuroinflammation and neurocognitive impairment.

evidence.reference_title: Brain microglia serve as a persistent HIV reservoir despite durable antiretroviral therapy.
evidence.snippet: These data demonstrate that MG harbor replication-competent HIV and serve as a persistent reservoir in the brain.

evidence.reference_title: Neuropathogenesis of HIV: from initial neuroinvasion to HIV-associated neurocognitive disorder (HAND).
evidence.snippet: Recent work suggests that the stage for HIV neuropathogenesis may be set with initial viral entry into the CNS, followed by initiation of pathogenetic processes including neuroinflammation and neurotoxicity, and establishment of local, compartmentalized HIV replication that may reflect a tissue reservoir for HIV.

nodes.description: Multiple host restriction factors inhibit HIV replication through distinct molecular mechanisms, but are counteracted by viral accessory proteins (Vif, Vpu, Nef).
